name: Loretta Sainte-Claire
role: Informal triage coordinator, St. Claude Bridge camp
publicFace: A composed, authoritative woman in her late forties who has been managing eighty people for five days without visible panic; survivors defer to her because she has not been wrong yet
privateGoal: Keep the population count stable and the resource gap invisible long enough for organized relief to arrive — and get Marcus Fontenot's deed documents into the legal record before the city starts moving on blighted properties
fear: That Renard Tureaud's faction will fracture the camp's cohesion at the exact moment relief assets arrive, creating a distribution crisis that kills someone who would otherwise have survived
knowledge: The actual Community_Resource_Reserve is 380 fluid ounces — not the 440 she announced this morning; the gap is a managed perception, not a mistake
The 24 MREs under the southern maintenance panel have not been disclosed to the general population
Renard Tureaud's eight-person faction has been organizing since yesterday afternoon and is waiting for a specific moment to move on the skiff
Two survivors in the third cluster have open floodwater wounds that Celestine Arceneaux has been monitoring without formal announcement
voice: Flat, precise, economical — she says exactly what is needed and stops; she does not reassure, she informs
trustLogic: Opens to anyone who demonstrates they have already done the arithmetic and are not asking her to pretend the numbers are different than they are; shuts down immediately when someone introduces a variable she cannot quantify
secrets: She has already decided that if Renard Tureaud moves on the skiff before relief arrives, she will let him take it — because the act of taking will isolate his faction from the rest of the camp more effectively than any confrontation
She knows the bridge deck's load rating and she knows the population is currently four people over the distributed-weight threshold; she has not told anyone

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Answers questions with questions — forces the questioner to articulate what they actually need before she responds
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Goes very still and very quiet; her voice drops rather than rises, which is more frightening than shouting
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not admit the load rating calculation; will not admit she has been managing the water figure
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Gives accurate information if she determines that withholding it will cost more than disclosing it — purely utilitarian
